title.rendered: What’s On the Travel Agenda for 2023?

content.rendered:
The travel industry is predicting that 2023 will see international trips and holidays back to full force as travellers across the world finally start to put the COVID crisis behind them.

The Travel Predictions 2023 survey has shown that travellers from around the world have changed from cautious and hopeful to bold and ready to put COVID in the past and resume their travel dreams.

The US, Australia and China have opened up, with business and leisure travel back to usual volumes. The Middle East has received a huge publicity boost with the successful World Cup in Qatar. This has put neighbouring destinations like Dubai, Abu Dhabi and even various cities in Saudi Arabia well and truly on the travel radar for many.

After years of uncertainty and staying close to home, many are looking for adventure, exploration and escapism. 50% of travellers want a complete culture shock for their 2023 holidays.

A massive 73% said they wanted a trip that would take them out of their comfort zones and pushing them to their limits.

Where would this be for you? Aboard the Trans-Siberian railway? Trekking in the Himalayas? Or a gastronomical tour of India? The choices are endless.

Eco friendly and off-the-grid style holidays have emerged as a popular choice for many. Though back to nature getaways, wilderness survival camps and earthly stays don’t mean roughing it. 48% said they would love this type of trip, but with luxuries and indulgences they deserve on holiday. And, despite many saying they wanted to completely switch off from work, 53% said they could not do without their phones or an internet connection on their off the beaten track, wilderness getaways.

Millennials, many of whom now have small children, are looking for trips down memory lane or back to iconic times – visiting retro hotels and destinations made famous in the 80s and 90s, or on old movies. Budva in Montenegro and Bozano in Italy are emerging as favourites. And they are planning to take their families with them on these era-themed holidays. A massive 54% of travellers said they would like to combine family reunions with their travel plans or arrange multi-generational trips in 2023.

Health and wellbeing retreats are popular travel options for many this year with 44% of travellers saying they will seek out mind, yoga, meditation and mindfulness getaways. India, Spain, Thailand and various remote locations in the US are popular destinations for this kind of hideaway.

Whatever you choose to do to relaunch your travel dreams this year, don’t forget to use a reliable and trusted currency company for your travel money.  We can supply you with travel money in 45 different currencies with the best rates available.

Happy new year and happy travelling!

Buy US Dollars – https://www.travelfx.co.uk/buy-us-dollars

Buy Euros – https://www.travelfx.co.uk/buy-euros

Buy UAE Dirham – https://www.travelfx.co.uk/buy-uae-dirham

Buy Thai Bhat – https://www.travelfx.co.uk/buy-thai-baht

See all currencies – https://www.travelfx.co.uk/latest-travel-money-rates

itemListElement.name: What’s On the Travel Agenda for 2023?

@graph.name: Travel FX Blog
@graph.description: Travel money news and tips from Travel FX

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
